Who I Am

About Me

My journey has never been a straight line; it has been a beautiful, deliberate exploration—trying, feeling, listening, letting each experience shape who I am becoming. As a restless child, Ajji channeled my energy into a drawing class that taught me patience and stillness.

Later, I fell in love with Bharatanatyam, breathing life into a dream my mother cherished until it became a huge part of my identity. A volunteer training program then blossomed into a deep love for nature—mountain treks, a camera lens, the quiet patience of bird watching. That same desire to observe and understand eventually led me to the most beautiful creation of all: the human mind, and the mysteries of why we become who we are.

Today, I work across two active roles — at The Eleos Project, where I facilitate workshops and training programs in soft skills, life skills, and foundational leadership for students; and at SwastiVishwa Behavioural and Social Sciences Institute Foundation, where I'm involved in the end-to-end execution of institutional training programs, counselling services, psychometric tool development, and research.

I am Tanishka Kulkarni, an Industrial-Organizational Psychologist specializing in employee well-being, facilitation, training and development, psychometrics, and organization development. Deeply passionate about applying every facet of behavioral science to optimize human potential and build thriving workplace environments. Always on my toes, eager to see where the next curiosity leads.

Expertise

Areas of Specialization

Training & Development

Designing and facilitating evidence-based training programs focused on soft skills, life skills, and leadership development for students and professionals.

Psychometrics

Development and application of psychometric tools to assess individual and group-level psychological constructs within organizational contexts.

Workshop Facilitation

Dynamic facilitation of interactive workshops covering foundational leadership, interpersonal effectiveness, and institutional development.

Organizational Research

Conducting applied behavioral and social science research to inform evidence-based organizational decisions and program design.

HR Operations Support

Supporting recruitment, employee feedback analysis, and reference background checks — translating HR data into meaningful organizational insight.

Employee & Student Well-being

Supporting institutional well-being initiatives — designing structured support frameworks, case-based learning, and psychosocial programs within organizational and educational settings.
